chunks.text:
Instructions for Submitting Assignment Booklets

1. Submit Assignment Booklets regularly for correction.
2. Submit only one Assignment Booklet at one time. This allows your teacher to
provide helpful comments that you can apply to subsequent course work and
exams (if applicable).
3. Check the following before submitting each Assignment Booklet:
a. Are all assignments complete?
b. Have you edited your work to ensure accuracy of information and details?
c. Have you proofread your work to ensure correct grammar, spelling, and
punctuation?
d. Did you complete the Assignment Booklet cover and attach the correct label?

Postal Mail
Determine sufficient postage by having the envelope weighed at a post office.
(Envelopes less than two centimetres thick receive the most economical rate.)

Alternate Methods
Contact your teacher if you require an alternate method for submission of assignments.

chunks.text:
Personal Psychology 20
Assignment Booklet 2
Assessment

For successful completion of this course, you must

1.

Complete all questions in each Assignment Booklet to the best of your ability.

2.

Achieve at least 40% on the final exam.

3. Achieve a final course mark of at least 50%.

Process
•
Read the course material
 before working on the activities in this Assignment
Booklet.  The Module Book contains the information necessary for successful
completion of your work.

•
Proceed carefully
through each assignment.  Reflect upon your answers and prepare
your written responses to communicate your thoughts effectively.  Time spent in
planning results in better writing.

•
Proofread your work
 before submitting it for marking.  Check for content,
organization, paragraph construction (if applicable), grammar, spelling, and
punctuation.

•
If you encounter difficulties or have any questions
, contact your course teacher at
Alberta Distance Learning Centre for assistance.

Format
If you choose to
 use a word processor
for your written work,
• format your work using an easy-to-read 12-point serif font such as Times New Roman
• include your full name and student file number as a document header
• double-space your final copy
• staple your printed work to this Assignment Booklet

Avoid plagiarism
 by acknowledging all sources you use.  Contact your teacher if you are
  uncertain how to document sources.

  Although you are encouraged to work collaboratively and discuss various aspects of this
  course with others,
all submitted work must be your own
.

Our Pledge to Students

Alberta Distance Learning Centre is committed to helping students achieve their educational goals.
We look forward to assisting students who are sincere in their desire to learn.  Students may contact
their course teacher(s) by phone, e-mail, fax, postal mail, or in person at an Alberta Distance Learning
Centre office.

chunks.text:
Personal Psychology 20  Assignment Booklet 2

ADLC Personal Psychology 20  1

Value
 17
Part One:  Multiple-Choice Items

Read each item carefully and decide which choice best completes the statement or answers the
question.  Write the letter of your choice in the blank before the item.

 _B__ 1. In the _____ stage of development, language is at a rudimentary level.

 p16  A.  1
st
 (prenatal)
   B.  2
nd
 (infancy)
   C.  3
rd
 (early childhood
   D.  4
th
 (late childhood)

 _D__ 2. Stage 7 (middle age), according to the course materials, has an age range between

   A.  30-50
 p16  B.  35-55
   C.  40-60
   D.  45-65

 Use the information below to answer multiple-choice item 3.

If a child is kept in a dark room for the first five years of life, he or she may never see even if
he or she was born with properly functioning eyes and a healthy visual cortex (part of the
brain).

 _C__ 3. The time span of five years is an example of _____ whereby the visual system
must be _____ or else it will cease to function.

 p16-  A.  a teratogen; exposed
 17  B.  prehension; irradiated
   C.  a critical period; stimulated
   D.  stage 3 (early childhood); activated

 _D__ 4. With descriptive research, the researcher _____ influence the participants and
_____ make an inference as to what might cause the observed behaviours.

 p17  A.  does;  does
   B.  does;  does not
   C.  does not;  does
   D.  does not;  does not
chunks.titleGuess: ALBERTA DISTANCE LEARNING CENTRE SSN2171 Personal Psychology 20 KEY

chunks.text:
Assignment Booklet 2  Personal Psychology 20
2  ADLC Personal Psychology 20

 _C__ 5. What type of study design would be most appropriate to investigate if people’s
problem-solving abilities increase as they age?

 p19  A.  Historical
   B.  Normative
   C.  Longitudinal
   D.  Cross-sectional

_A__ 6. What type of study design would be most appropriate to determine if lying was a
genetically inherited trait?

 p21  A.  Twin
   B.  Historical
   C.  Correlational
   D.  Cross-sectional

_A__ 8. What type of study design would be most appropriate to determine if the reported
cases of birth defects have decreased over the last century?

 p19  A.  Historical
   B.  Normative
   C.  Longitudinal
   D.  Cross-sectional

 _D__ 8. What type of study design would be most appropriate to determine if boys in
three age categories have denser bones than girls in the same age categories?

 p20  A.  Twin
   B.  Historical
   C.  Correlational
   D.  Cross-sectional

 _B__ 9. What type of study design would be most appropriate to determine when most
children learn to walk?

 p18  A.  Historical
   B.  Normative
   C.  Longitudinal
   D.  Cross-sectional
chunks.titleGuess: ALBERTA DISTANCE LEARNING CENTRE SSN2171 Personal Psychology 20 KEY

chunks.text:
Personal Psychology 20  Assignment Booklet 2

ADLC Personal Psychology 20  3

 Use the information below to answer multiple-choice item 10.

A set of identical twins are separated at birth.  As adults the twins are reunited and studied by
researchers.  The researchers learn that one twin is much more aggressive than the other.

 _B__ 10. Using their knowledge of identical twins, the researchers theorize that the
difference in aggressiveness is due to

 p21  A.  genetic differences
   B.  environmental factors
   C.  which twin was born first
   D.  how old the twins were when reunited

 _A__ 11. When a sperm cell fertilizes and egg cell, the resulting cluster of cells is called
a(n) _____ for the first _____ weeks after fertilization.

 p22  A.  zygote; two
   B.  zygote; eight
   C.  embryo; two
   D.  embryo; eight

 _B__ 12. The results of a(n) _____ is more _____ because the doctor must interpret and
determine the relevance of certain facial expressions.

 p28  A.  Prechtl Test; objective
   B.  Prechtl Test; subjective
   C.  Apgar Exam; objective
   D.  Apgar Exam; subjective

 Use the information below to answer multiple-choice item 13.

A doctor uses the Apgar exam to assess a newborn.  The infant is rated a zero on stimulus
response, a one on muscle tone, and the best rating in the rest of the categories.

 _C__ 13. This infant would have an overall rating of
 p28
   A.  five
   B.  six
   C.  seven
   D.  eight
chunks.titleGuess: ALBERTA DISTANCE LEARNING CENTRE SSN2171 Personal Psychology 20 KEY

chunks.text:
Assignment Booklet 2  Personal Psychology 20
4  ADLC Personal Psychology 20

 _A__ 14. The Brazelton scale is a comprehensive and time consuming neonatal test
because the test assesses a baby’s _____ behaviours such as sucking and _____.

 p29  A.  organized; crying
   B.  repetitive ; sleeping
   C.  controlled; breathing
   D.  coordinated; thermoregulation

 Use the information below to answer multiple-choice items 15 and 16.

Robbie is two years-old when his parents purchase a one month old Pomeranian dog.  This is
the first dog Robbie is introduced to and when his parents call the Pomeranian “doggie,”
Robbie calls the next five small furry four-legged animals he sees, “doggie.”

 _A__ 15. By calling the next five small furry four-legged animals he sees, “doggie,”
Robbie is experiencing

 p34  A.  overextension
   B.  underextension
   C.  overgeneralization
   D.  undergeneralization

 _B__ 16. Robbie is then introduced to an adult St. Bernard dog.  He does not call this dog
“doggie” which means he is experiencing

 p34  A.  overextension
   B.  underextension
   C.  overgeneralization
   D.  undergeneralization

 _D__ 17. While stereotypes may be positive or negative, they are all _____images or ideas
regarding a particular type of person or thing.

 p41  A.  neutral
   B.  unbiased
   C.  complicated
   D.  oversimplified

chunks.text:
Personal Psychology 20  Assignment Booklet 2

ADLC Personal Psychology 20  5

Value
10
Part Two:  Matching Teratogen to Effect

In the blank beside each statement, place the letter of the term (teratogen) that best describes
the effect.  Terms may not be used more than once and not all terms will be used.  Please note
the category of teratogen in the statement when responding.

p24-25

Statements

Terms
 _H__ 1. Consumption of this therapeutic drug may
increase the likelihood of heart defects.
A.   alcohol
   B.   barbiturates
    C.   gold
 _L__ 2. Hearing loss is one potential effect of this
therapeutic drug.
D.   herpes
   E.   lead
    F.   mercury
 _E__ 3. Exposure to this environmental hazard may
cause anemia.
G.   nicotine (smoking)
   H.     phenytoin
    I. PCB*
 _N__ 4. Discoloration of teeth is common with this
therapeutic drug.
J.   radiation
   K.   rubella
    L.   streptomycin
 _F__ 5. Exposure to this environmental hazard may
result in poor motor coordination.
M.   syphilis
   N.   tetracycline
    O.   thalidomide
 _B__ 6. Respiratory problems may occur in newborns
whose mothers have used this therapeutic drug.

 _J__ 7. Leukemia, genetic alterations, and cancer may
result because of exposure to this environmental
hazard.

 _A__ 8. Excessive and continued use of this substance by
the mother may result in brain damage, growth
retardation, and mental retardation.

   * PCB
polychlorinated biphenyl
 _D__ 9. This infection in the mother may cause eye
damage, hearing loss, and heart defects in the
newborn.

 __O_ 10. Deformed limbs are a common effect of this
therapeutic drug.
chunks.titleGuess: ALBERTA DISTANCE LEARNING CENTRE SSN2171 Personal Psychology 20 KEY

chunks.text:
Assignment Booklet 2  Personal Psychology 20
6  ADLC Personal Psychology 20

Value
10
Part Three:  True or False

Read each item carefully and decide if the statement is true or false.  Write T or F in the
blanks to indicate your choices.

 _F__ 1. In a healthy newborn, the last sense to fully mature is taste.

p26

 _T__ 2. At first, babies recognize their parents by their voices and smell, not by sight.
 p27

 _T__ 3. Correlations are relationships that exist between two factors/variables.
 p17

 _F__ 4. Normative studies judge a child as being either normal or abnormal with respect
to an average child.
 p18

 _T__ 5. Cognitive language theorists believe that children learn the meaning of words
through classification and categorization.
 p31

 _F__ 6. Social inferences are the sets of steps a child must follow when performing a
given behaviour.
 p40

 _F__ 7. Prehension relates to a baby’s grasping reflex.
 p27

 _T__ 8. Modelling is a way parents encourage a child’s proper use of words.
 p35

 _F__ 9. Punishment is the main influence on a child’s social development.
 p39

 _F_ 10. Motor development proceeds in a toe-to-head fashion.
 p27
chunks.titleGuess: ALBERTA DISTANCE LEARNING CENTRE SSN2171 Personal Psychology 20 KEY

chunks.text:
Personal Psychology 20  Assignment Booklet 2

ADLC Personal Psychology 20  7

Value
13
Part Four:  Written-Response Items

Respond to the following items on the blanks below the items.

4
1. How do longitudinal studies differ from cross-sectional studies?

p19 - 20

Longitudinal research studies the same people over a long period of time. (2)

Cross-sectional research uses many different age groups all at once.  (2)

2
2. What is the palmar reflex?

p27
The palmar reflex is the grasping reflex babies have when an object is placed

into the palms of their hands.

2
3. The course materials describe three methods of defining temperament.  Select one of
the methods and list the areas that are assessed.

1.  New York Longitudinal Study assesses 9 areas; activity, rhythmicity, intensity,

p38
approach-withdrawal, adaptability, threshold, distractibility, mood, and attention

span persistence.

2.  Emotionality, Activity, and Sociability Model assesses emotionality, activity, and

Sociability.

3. Rothbarts Model assesses reactivity and self-regulation.
chunks.titleGuess: ALBERTA DISTANCE LEARNING CENTRE SSN2171 Personal Psychology 20 KEY

chunks.text:
Assignment Booklet 2  Personal Psychology 20
8  ADLC Personal Psychology 20

3
4. The following are some stereotypes you may have heard before:

- Asian people are smart (e.g., good at math).
- Blondes are dumb.
- Teenagers are not trustworthy.
- Jewish people are frugal.
- Albertans (especially oil-field/rig-workers) are “red-necks.”

Provide two additional examples of stereotypes that you have heard or might hold.
Examples may include references to people in your geographical area, culture, or age
group and/or they may include stereotypes of Americans, Canadians, Ukrainians,
Caucasians, Francophones, etc.

Answers will vary.  This is a “gimmie” question.  1.5 marks per example.

2
5. Using one of your examples from question four, explain why the stereotype could not
possibly apply to all the members of the group.

p42
Answers will vary, but students should indicate that some people will not have

the characteristic indicated by the stereotype they have listed.

End of Assignment Booklet 2
After you have completed all items, submit your work to your marker.
